ops.title: Live state of the cookiesyncâ†’Go rewrite onto a shared synckit module; repo locations and the local-replace / don't-push constraint

ops.body:
The [[cookiesync-architecture]] rewrite (cookiesync Python â†’ Go, aligned with reposync on a shared `synckit` Go module) is being executed phase-by-phase. Repo locations on this Mac:

- **reposync**: `~/Code/repo-sync` (module `github.com/yasyf/reposync`)
- **synckit** (NEW): `~/Code/synckit` (module `github.com/yasyf/synckit`) â€” PUBLISHED at github.com/yasyf/synckit (public, repo-bootstrapped as a Go *library*: no cmd/ binary, no goreleaser; CI green; fox-kit mascot + GitHub social-preview set; session reviewer + cc-notes armed; `reposync repo add`ed). reposync still imports it via local `replace => ../synckit`, NOT the remote require.
- **cookiesync**: `~/Code/cookie-sync` (module `github.com/yasyf/cookiesync`) â€” now a Go CLI (Phase 3 in-place Pythonâ†’Go rewrite, in progress); `replace => ../synckit`

**Phase 0 DONE** (2026-06-23): `hostregistry` extracted from reposync into synckit, parameterized over `hostregistry.Config{Name}`. reposync imports synckit via `replace github.com/yasyf/synckit => ../synckit` in its go.mod.

**Phase 1 DONE** (2026-06-23): the generic substrate is lifted into synckit as 4 cycles (each its own commit pair): `synckit/service` (AgentSpec/ToolConfig/LaunchdManager), `synckit/codec` (Duration) + `hostregistry.Config.UpdateRaw` (FK-preserving; reposync dropped duplicated State.Self/Hosts), `synckit/rpc` ({method,params} + peer-UID via GetsockoptXucred + 16 MiB max-line + 30s/10m timeouts; CLI/SSH contract frozen), `synckit/watch` (generic anti-echo `Engine[T]`). Both repos green (`go test -race` + golangci-lint); golden intact. Validated live on the real Mac: reinstalled the new binary, confirmed `install`+`reconcile`+RPC socket work, then restored the original brew daemon (v0.6.1); state.json byte-identical after. Local commit tips: reposync `a461d44d`, synckit `020adc5c`. synckit packages now: codec, hostregistry, rpc, service, watch.

**Phase 2 DONE** (2026-06-23): `synckit/cregistry` LWW-Element-Set CRDT (e26daaf5, pushed, adversarially proven â€” 18k merge-orderings, zero divergence) + `synckit/converge` (Driver[V]/Fetcher/pull-only Reconcile, loop-guard proven; synckit tip 0461fe2a, pushed). reposync adopted it (tip c072d19a, LOCAL): RepoDriver over a clean convergent state.json (`repos` = cregistry.Registry keyed by origin; `local_repos` keyed by relpath, never sent to peers), ssh Fetcher via daemon-independent `reposync state get-json`, and the **removal-propagation FIX** (repo rm writes a removed_at tombstone that pull-merges to peers â†’ converge to absent). Both modules green. Live cross-host convergence test deferred to cutover (state wiped, nothing registered yet).

**Phase 3 DONE** (2026-06-24) â€” in-place Pythonâ†’Go rewrite of github.com/yasyf/cookiesync at `~/Code/cookie-sync` (it IS the cookiesync repo; Python preserved by tag v0.2.0 + PyPI). go module `github.com/yasyf/cookiesync`, `replace => ../synckit`, mirrors reposync. SQLite = **modernc.org/sqlite (pure-Go)**. Domain in `internal/{cookie,helper,cache,state,engine,daemon,watch,cli,service}`. Commits (LOCAL, UNPUSHED â€” no push during rewrite): 3.0 convert `414f9484`; 3.1 crypto `5a936c62`; 3.2 sqlite `a028e0bb`; 3.3 merge/serialize/getcookie `2660b68b` (2 real parity bugs caught+fixed: big.Rat expiry, getcookie 2/443 defaults); 3.4 helper+consent+cache `b90e8931` (Touch ID fix: always-set reason, single prompt; compose_reason C0-sep whitespace fix); 3.5 sync engine `d4028833` (convergent endpoint registry, pipeline, value-union converge, Driver, ssh backend, rpc extract/apply); 3.6 daemon+RPC+routed-consent `f6238db0` (9 methods, nonce/endpoint binding, ioreg whoami); 3.7 watch+CLI `baf12973` (synckit watch anti-echo + browser/install/uninstall/doctor); cleanup `fa71c60b`. synckit got an additive watch.Engine.Seed (`52408ed1`, PUSHED, CI green). EVERY cycle adversarially parity-verified vs the recovered Python oracle (byte-exact crypto/sqlite/merge/serialize/pipeline/converge/digest; routed-consent security; anti-echo termination; CLI surface verbatim). Binary builds, 15 cmds, no stubs, signed helper `/Applications/cookiesync-keyhelper.app` contract probe OK. Verification pattern: recover deleted Python via `git show 414f9484~1:cookiesync/...` and run it (`uv run --with cryptography`/`aiosqlite`) as a byte-exact oracle. WORKFLOW NOTE: big implementer agents trip the StructuredOutput report cap (work lands fine) â€” use LEAN implementer schemas + direct-subagent verification.

**Phase 4 ~DONE â€” LIVE CUTOVER COMPLETE (2026-06-24).** Both Macs migrated to the Go stack and verified live: reposync 0.7.0 (mesh re-established selfâ†”yasyf-home, 32 repos re-added from the wipe-backup and **converged to yasyf-home via pull-merge** â€” Phase 2b validated on real hardware); cookiesync Go daemons up + stable on both, identical 3-endpoint registries (yasyf@yasyf:chrome:Profile 3, yasyf@yasyf:arc:Default, yasyf@yasyf-home:chrome:Default). **Live smoke PASSED**: `cookiesync auth` (one clean Touch ID) + `cookiesync cookies github.com` decrypted the real authenticated session and emitted valid Playwright JSON â€” whole stack (consent/SE/crypto/sqlite/serialize) works. **TWO bugs the cutover surfaced, both fixed+committed+CI-green**: (1) `browser add` validated hosts against cookiesync's own empty registry â†’ now bridges to reposync via the new `internal/mesh.Resolve` (`reposync host ls --json`); (2) the engine/daemon/watch derived self+peers from cookiesync's own (empty-on-fresh-host) state â†’ also via `mesh.Resolve` (a fresh host couldn't bootstrap convergence). cookiesync commits past v0.3.0: browser-add fix 25b9f77, mesh fix 429580a, test fix 8e77a0f. **OPEN ITEM: the v0.3.2 brew release is BLOCKED on an Apple notary outage** (Post .../notary/v2/submissions deadline-exceeded; v0.3.0/reposync-0.7.0 notarized fine earlier). The v0.3.2 tag exists; re-run the failed release job (`gh run rerun --failed`) when Apple recovers, then `brew upgrade cookiesync` on both Macs. UNTIL THEN the deployed cookiesync binary is a LOCAL `go build` copied over /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/cookiesync/0.3.0/cookiesync (works â€” the CLI just shells the signed helper); do NOT `brew reinstall cookiesync` (would revert to v0.3.0). Endpoints were registered directly on both hosts (daemon-independent browser add) since the daemon needs â‰Ą1 endpoint to avoid idle churn. Old Python cookiesync (uv tool) uninstalled both hosts.

**Phase 4 (release + cutover).** DONE: synckit **v0.1.0** tagged+pushed; reposync converted replaceâ†’`require synckit v0.1.0`, pushed (origin/main `cf92969`, **CI green**); cookiesync converted replaceâ†’require + published the Go rewrite (origin/main `63919c3`, **CI green** â€” its `test` job runs on **macos-latest** since cookiesync is macOS-only). **Helper relocated off /Applications â†’ the Homebrew Caskroom**: the `cookiesync-keyhelper` cask is now `stage_only true` (pushed to yasyf/homebrew-tap); `internal/paths/helper.go` resolves it by globbing `<brew-prefix>/Caskroom/cookiesync-keyhelper/*/cookiesync-keyhelper.app` (+ `$COOKIESYNC_HELPER` dev override). (Caution learned: a cask `app` stanza MOVES the .app to /Applications and leaves a Caskroom *symlink* â€” `rm`ing /Applications deletes the real bundle; recover with `brew reinstall --cask cookiesync-keyhelper`, the signed zip is on the v0.1.0 GH release.) RELEASE DONE: cookiesync `.goreleaser.yaml` (binary cask, darwin-only, CGO off) + `.github/workflows/release.yml` (helper sign/notarize/cask-publish ported from release-pypi.yml; PyPI dropped; BOTH tap pushes use `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN` â€” this repo lacks reposync's `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN`; goreleaser runs on macos-15 for the native notarize block). **Tagged + released cookiesync v0.3.0** â€” the `cookiesync` cask is in the tap (depends_on cask cookiesync-keyhelper; darwin amd64+arm64 notarized binaries); `brew install yasyf/tap/cookiesync` works. The in-CI helper Developer-ID signing job is gated on helper SOURCE changes (`detect-helper-changes` excludes `helper/build/`, which is now gitignored + removed from the repo â€” it was committed build output that would have spuriously triggered re-signing). **DEFERRED â€” needs the user PRESENT + screen UNLOCKED (Touch ID + SE keygen; the daemon's cache-newkey exits 2 / SecKeyCreateRandomKey fails when locked):** the live smoke (build, run `cookiesync watch` daemon, `auth`+`cookies` against live Chrome Profile 3), the full deploy on both Macs (bootout old `com.github.yasyf.cookiesync.{watch,reconcile}` â†’ install new), and re-registering hosts/repos/browsers. Old Python cookiesync was at `~/.local/bin/cookiesync` (pip); its agents already booted out here.

**FOLLOW-ON REWRITE â€” synckitd multi-consumer daemon (STARTED 2026-06-25).** A distinct, NEW rewrite on top of the finished Go stack: collapse the per-tool daemons into ONE generic daemon `synckitd` (new `cmd/synckitd` + `daemon/` package in synckit) that owns the single shared host mesh (`hostregistry.Mesh = Config{Name:"synckit"}` â†’ `~/.config/synckit/`), watch supervisor, reconcile tick, and rpc socket, reading a JSON manifest per consumer and SHELLING OUT to each consumer's CLI (`list --json`/`reconcile`/`sync --origin`/`state get-json`/`state apply-json`). Motivation: the live cutover surfaced that cookiesync had no mesh of its own and shelled `reposync host ls --json` (`internal/mesh/mesh.go` â€” deleted by this work). Architecture + contract doc: cc-notes doc `e48a6a7` ("Multi-consumer daemon rewrite"). Plan: `~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-03/plans/found-the-bug-graceful-rain.md`. Progress (all automated work DONE + green: build/vet/golangci-lint(0)/`go test -race` across all three repos, plus a local integration smoke where synckitd discovers+drives both real consumer manifests): P1 synckit substrate (Mesh+MigrateLegacyMesh, manifest [JSON], cliconverge, watchbackend, service.AgentSpec.Binary); P2 synckitd daemon (cmd/synckitd + daemon pkg: serve/reconcile-tick/host/bootstrap/register/status/install, one rpc socket, watch supervisor); P3a cookiesync (resident `helper-serve` keeps SE cache+consent+9 RPC methods; new `list --json` + `state apply-json`; internal/mesh.Resolve now reads hostregistry.Mesh â€” the reposync shell-out BUG IS GONE; internal/{watch,service} deleted); P3b reposync (new `list --json` + `state apply-json`; `reconcile --origin`; deleted internal/{watch,rpc,service,host}; `host ls --json` shimmed to `synckitd host ls`). **CORRECTNESS FIX I caught in review:** both manifests' sync action must use `--origin {{.Origin}}` (= the NOTIFIER's self), NOT `{{.Peer}}` â€” synckitd passes both Peer=peer and Origin=self to manifest.Render; cookiesync's Converge PUSHES the union to peers in the notifier's own local pass, so the peer-notify must skip the notifier (origin=self) to prevent echo, matching both original notifiers (`--origin <self>`). **RELEASED + PUSHED (2026-06-25):** all three repos committed (jj) and pushed, CI green on each. **synckit v0.2.0** tagged â†’ goreleaser publishes the `synckitd` binary (Developer-ID signed+notarized via quill on the ubuntu runner) as **`Formula/synckitd.rb`** in yasyf/homebrew-tap â†’ `brew install yasyf/tap/synckitd`. synckit gained `.goreleaser.yaml` + `.github/workflows/release.yml` + `.github/formula/synckitd.rb.tmpl` (the CANONICAL repo-bootstrap formula pattern, NOT reposync's older `homebrew_casks`: goreleaser builds/notarizes, the workflow renders the formula from `dist/checksums.txt` and publishes via the shared `yasyf/homebrew-tap/.github/actions/publish@main`). Secrets `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN` + the five `MACOS_*` were set on the synckit repo via `repo-bootstrap/scripts/set-release-secrets.sh yasyf/synckit` (reads 1Password vault OpenClaw; `op` was signed in). Consumers stripped the dev-time `replace` â†’ **`require github.com/yasyf/synckit v0.2.0`**, pushed (CI green), then released **cookiesync v0.4.0** + **reposync v0.8.0** casks. (`GOFLAGS=-mod=mod` precludes `go.work`, so the dev wiring was a `replace`, now removed.) Apple notary worked this run (the earlier v0.3.2 outage cleared). STILL DEFERRED (needs user present + both Macs unlocked, ssh, Touch ID â€” task P4-live): the live two-host deploy â€” `brew upgrade` both Macs, `synckitd install` (loads serve+reconcile + the cookiesync helper Aqua agent; boots out legacy per-tool agents), `synckitd host add user@peer` (bootstrap + legacy-mesh migration via MigrateLegacyMesh) â€” then the cross-host smoke (one cookie change â†’ one converge, no echo; one repo trunk â†’ one converge; one Touch ID per window).

**TYPED RPC CONTRACT + LIVE TWO-HOST DEPLOY (2026-06-27).** Replaced the stringly-typed synckitdâ†”consumer CLI contract (manifest `actions{}` + `watch.list_cmd` argv templates, shelled + stdout-parsed) with a TYPED RPC service. New `synckit/syncservice` pkg: `SyncConsumer` interface (Capabilities/List/Reconcile/Sync/GetState, `svc.`-namespaced over the existing rpc pkg), typed `Client` over 3 transports (Socket / Stdio / SSHStdio), `ProtocolVersion=1` handshake (fails loud on skew). rpc gained `ServeConn` (streaming loop) / `CallRaw` / `Proxy` (byte-exact stdinâ†”socket bridge) / exported `ReadLine`; manifest `actions`â†’`service{transport,serve_args,sock}`; daemon drives consumers over the typed Client with async, bounded-retry engine startup; deleted `converge/cliconverge` + `daemon/shell.go`. **apply_state DROPPED** (no caller â€” converge writes the merged registry IN-PROCESS via the native `Driver.SaveRegistry`; only GetState crosses hosts; PULL-ONLY *is* the loop guard). Registry payload stays opaque `json.RawMessage` so int64 cregistry stamps round-trip byte-exact â€” NEVER decode through `map[string]any`/float64. cookiesync: resident helper serves svc.* on its socket (warm SE key stays resident); new `cookiesync rpc-serve` = thin `rpc.Proxy` bridge to that socket (NOT a fresh `daemon.Build` â€” no SE re-prime); registry SSHFetcher â†’ typed GetState over ssh-stdio; value-union `rpc extract`/`apply` fleet protocol UNTOUCHED. reposync: `reposync rpc-serve` = long-lived stdio `ServeConn` (per-request `state.Load`; List covers Repos[id=origin]+LocalRepos[id=relpath]; GetState=`EncodeRepoRegistry`, Repos-only). **RELEASED as a matched set, all CI+release green: synckit v0.3.0 â†’ cookiesync v0.5.0 â†’ reposync v0.9.0.** Release flow: dev uses local `replace => /Users/yasyf/Code/synckit` (stripped at release); tag synckit FIRST (module resolution), then `go get synckit@v0.3.0` + tidy in consumers, then tag them; tag push â†’ release.yml. cookiesync helper-build/notarize runs ONLY when `helper/` changed since the prev tag â€” it hasn't since v0.1.0, so the **cookiesync-keyhelper cask stays at 0.1.0** (the current signed/notarized helper; compatible with any cookiesync that doesn't touch helper/).

**LIVE DEPLOY DONE on both Macs (yasyf + yasyf-home).** Found synckitd had NEVER been deployed (no ~/.config/synckit; both Macs still on the legacy per-tool agents; this Mac was even behind at cookiesync 0.3.0/reposync 0.7.0 while the peer was already 0.5.0/0.9.0). Clean cutover on both: booted out + rm'd the 4 legacy agents (`com.github.yasyf.{cookiesync,reposync}.{watch,reconcile}`), `brew install yasyf/tap/synckitd` + keyhelper, `brew upgrade --cask cookiesync reposync`, `cookiesync install` + `reposync install` (write `~/.config/synckit/manifests/*.json`), `synckitd install` (creates serve+reconcile+helper.cookiesync agents). Per user request, FULL-WIPED all state (cookiesync+reposync+synckit state.json + sockets/locks; manifests kept) on both, then `synckitd host add yasyf@yasyf-home` rebuilt the symmetric mesh. Both `cookiesync doctor` green except the expected empty-browsers; typed contract verified live (svc.listâ†’`[]`, svc.capabilities round-trip = "svc protocol v1"). PENDING: user re-adds browsers (`cookiesync browser add`) + repos (`reposync repo add`/`discover`), then the final cross-host smoke (repo smoke = no Touch ID; cookie smoke = one Touch ID).

**SHARED TUI + HOST-DISCOVERY FIXES (2026-06-27).** Surfaced while bringing the system up for the cross-host smoke (reposync's TUI exposed two `synckit/hostregistry` bugs; cookiesync had no TUI). Fixed at source + extracted the shared UI; **RELEASED as a matched set, all CI+release green: synckit v0.4.0 â†’ reposync v0.10.0 â†’ cookiesync v0.6.0** (synckit main tips: host-fix `c0c14c7`, tui `0a03905`; reposync `07d49629`; cookiesync `1f18187`). (a) **Bug B** â€” `hostregistry.Config.Name` was overloaded as BOTH the `~/.config/<Name>` dir AND the ssh install-probe binary; for the shared mesh that's `"synckit"` (not an installed binary) so a bootstrapped peer read "reachable, not installed". Added `Config.Binary` (+`const MeshBinary="synckitd"`, `Mesh=Config{Name:MeshName,Binary:MeshBinary}`); `Verify`/`RemoteInstalled` probe `c.Binary`, `store.go` keeps `c.Name` for the dir; `VerifyBinary`/`RemoteInstalledBinary` (daemon per-manifest) untouched. (b) **Bug A** â€” `discoverBonjour` advertised THIS Mac under its `.local` name, slipping past the tailscale self-skip (self's tailscale node â‰  its bonjour node); `Hosts` now resolves `scutil --get LocalHostName` via the Runner and drops the candidate whose `bonjourNode` matches (case-insensitive). (c) "registered" was **NOT a bug** â€” the symmetric yasyfâ†”yasyf-home mesh was the one rebuilt post-wipe. **Shared `synckit/tui` package** (NEW, gains charm deps bubbletea/bubbles/lipgloss; the synckitd binary NEVER imports it â€” verified `go list -deps ./cmd/...`): generic shell (tab router, master-detail, filter bar, theme) + the entire Hosts tab extracted from reposync. Public seam: `tui.Run(ctx, Options{Brand,Version,Screens,Runner})` always appends the shared Hosts tab; consumer content screens implement the exported `Screen` interface (Init/Updateâ†’Screen/View/Title/Help/WantsKey) + compose exported primitives (`MasterDetail`/`SplitDims`/`FilterBar`/`FilterItems`/style vars). I added `Options.Version` during seam review so neither header regresses. reposync keeps ONLY its Repos screen; **cookiesync GAINED its first TUI** â€” bare `cookiesync` on a TTY (gated by `term.IsTerminal`) or `cookiesync tui`: a Browsers tab + full-picker add (host via `mesh.Resolve` â†’ browser via `cookie.Registry` â†’ profile via NEW read-only `cookie.Browser.Profiles()` that scans DataRoot subdirs holding a Cookies store) + remove-with-confirm; `browser add/ls/rm` stays the non-interactive escape hatch (also the fallback for a remote-only profile the local FS can't scan). Sacred seams (value-union fleet `engine/ssh.go` extract/apply, SE/Touch-ID helper, `rpc-serve` bridge, daemon) UNTOUCHED; `helper/` untouched â†’ keyhelper cask stays 0.1.0 (release skipped helper-build/publish-cask). Release ordering unchanged (tag synckit FIRST; in consumers drop `replace => ../synckit`, `GOPRIVATE=github.com/yasyf/synckit go get synckit@v0.4.0` to skip proxy/sumdb lag, tidy, then tag; every per-repo tag pushed AFTER advancing `main` since the shared release CI enforces "Verify the tag is on main"). **STILL PENDING (P4, USER-PRESENT): `brew upgrade` both Macs to the new triple + `synckitd install`, verify the Hosts tab live (no self row = Bug A, peer reads "installed" = Bug B, mesh kept), then the cross-host smoke** â€” user re-adds a browser via the new cookiesync TUI; one cookie change â†’ one converge, no echo, one Touch ID; the test repo (user added one) â†’ one converge, no Touch ID.

**TRANSPORT RACE FIX (2026-07-03) â€” matched set synckit v0.7.1 / cookiesync v0.8.1 / reposync v0.11.1, all CI+release green.** The yasyf-home helper-daemon panic (29Ă— on 07-02, nil deref at syncservice/transport.go:117) was a race in synckit's `cmdTransport.Do`: on ctx expiry `reset()` nilled `t.in/t.out` under a still-running `exchange` goroutine. Fixed by snapshotting the pipes under mu + making `exchange` a free function; regression tests: fast ctx-timeout + a starved-scheduler soak (spinners + pre-expired ctx â€” the ONLY way the bad interleaving reproduces; naive timeout tests pass on broken code, and TSan misses the read-then-write direction because the fd-close creates a masking HB edge). Codex adversarial review: PASS on all dimensions. Repro+verify evidence: synckit cc-notes log `af1bd03`. DEPLOYED BINARIES STILL PRE-FIX until P4 brew-upgrade â€” the panic recurs on peer timeout until then (benign: launchd respawns).

**SESSION-REQUESTOR (2026-07-03) â€” cookiesync v0.8.2 released + deployed BOTH Macs, live-verified.** One Claude Code session = one consent requestor, no env needed: `internal/cli/requestor.go` `resolveRequestor` (COOKIESYNC_REQUESTOR wins verbatim â†’ ordered `agentSessions` list; Claude Code via `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID` â†’ `"Claude Code Â· "+shortRef` (pre-hyphen segment capped at 8)), forwarded from the 6 requestor-ladder sites (4 in cookies.go + rpc extract/get_cookies; `rpc request_consent` is host-keyed approver mode â€” excluded on purpose). Daemon untouched (req: token renders verbatim; reaches the helper as `COOKIESYNC_TOUCHID_REASON` env, no shell). Live-verified: first auth taps once with the sheet reading "â€¦for Claude Code Â· a3283ae1" (user-confirmed), repeats from FRESH login sessions are silent at **0.37â€“0.68s** (the warm path is NOT sub-100ms â€” the earlier 0.091s was a one-off; don't pin <100ms in future verifications), `COOKIESYNC_REQUESTOR=other` still taps (isolation). Skill `agent-browser-with-cookies` â†’ **0.6.1 docs-only** (one-session-one-tap Note + inline-prefix pattern for non-Claude runs): the originally-planned skill `export` was review-killed twice â€” an export in one skill step does NOT persist to the next Bash call, and a persisted env would OVERRIDE auto-detect with a full-UUID token and split grants. yasyf-home doctor key-cache FAIL (OSStatus -25308) during deploy = locked screen, benign ([[cookiesync-daemon-locked-screen]]). Go test gotcha: resolver tests must `t.Setenv` EVERY read var incl. `""` â€” the dev env itself exports CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID.

**BOTH MACS ON v0.10.3 (2026-07-12).** yasyf-home is NOT behind â€” it upgraded to 0.10.2 on Jul 11 21:01 and to 0.10.3 on Jul 12 (verified: fresh helper pid, doctor 7/7 OK). Any earlier "yasyf-home pending upgrade" note is stale. keyhelper cask 0.8.0 on both, not stuck. See [[cookiesync-union-hang-and-deadline-fixes]] for what v0.10.3 carries. **v0.10.4 RELEASED (2026-07-13)** â€” carries the auth_status heal-mutex fix (lock-free atomic readers) + the ioreg-only keybag probe â€” see [[cookiesync-union-hang-and-deadline-fixes]]. **v0.10.5 RELEASED + DEPLOYED THIS MAC (2026-07-13)** â€” webstorage partitioned-StorageKey fix (see [[cookiesync-web-storage]]); this Mac jumped 0.10.3â†’0.10.5 in one hop (picking up 0.10.4 too), **yasyf-home still 0.10.3, pending its brew upgrade**. DEPLOY GOTCHAS from that hop: `brew upgrade --cask cookiesync` refused ("cannot be upgraded as-is" â†’ use `brew reinstall --cask --force`), and the local tap clone was stale (`brew update` first or the reinstall installs the OLD version â€” it silently reinstalled 0.10.4 when the tap already had 0.10.5). After the binary swap the helper socket wedged (doctor: auth_status i/o timeout; launchctl kickstart alone did NOT heal it) â€” `synckitd install` fixed it exactly as doctor's remedy line says; its "Bootstrap failed: 5" on the already-loaded serve agent is benign noise. synckitd 0.10.0 landed on this Mac the same morning (separate session).

**BOTH MACS ON cookiesync 0.13.1 + synckitd 0.15.1 + ccx 0.17.3 (2026-07-14 PM â€” post-Arc-incident F7 pass; supersedes every earlier deployed-version note).** Release-tag gotcha from this hop: synckit v0.15.0 was tagged on the un-merged branch commit (bcdd8ef), the release gate ("Verify the tag is on main") refused it, and the tap silently stayed at 0.14.0 â€” the Go module proxy had already served v0.15.0 so the tag must NEVER move; the binary shipped as v0.15.1 from main (61af4b5). cookiesync v0.13.1 requires synckit v0.15.0 (module) while the deployed synckitd binary is 0.15.1 â€” same content. Watch releases to completion: the failure sat unnoticed for ~2h because the tagging session walked away.

**STATE LOCATIONS (corrects a user misconception):** only the host MESH moved to the shared `~/.config/synckit/state.json` (`{hosts,self}`). Per-tool DATA stays per-tool: `~/.config/cookiesync/state.json` (browser registry) + `~/.config/reposync/state.json` (repo registry). Wiping those loses the tracked browsers/repos (NOT a no-op).

**OPS GOTCHAS:** both Macs' login shell is FISH â†’ `ssh peer '<bash for-loop / (subshell)>'` FAILS (fish misparses `(...)` as command-substitution); scp a bash script + `ssh peer bash /tmp/x.sh` for multi-step remote ops (the brew-shellenv `eval "$(...)"` wrapper itself DOES run under fish). `synckitd install` over ssh bootstraps the Aqua helper agent fine when the peer is unlocked (GUI session present); the rpc sockets bind ~1s after install â€” don't check immediately (false "FAIL helper socket"). The Bash tool here runs bash (not the fish login shell), so local bash syntax is fine.

**Phase-3 HAZARD (load-bearing): the flock is NON-REENTRANT** (gofrs/flock BSD flock(2)). `converge.Reconcile` owns the lock for the whole pass, so any Driver method that re-enters `hostregistry.Config.WithLock`/`UpdateRaw`/`state.Update` inside the pass self-hangs (spins to ctx deadline â†’ ErrLockBusy). cookiesync's Driver (Phase 3) MUST use the lock-free `*Unlocked` write path (`hostregistry.Config.UpdateRawUnlocked`) or a pre-loaded state inside the converge pass â€” never `state.Update`. reposync's RepoDriver already does this.

**GREENFIELD decision (user, 2026-06-23): NO migration / backward-compat / legacy-parsing / sentinel code anywhere â€” there are no users.** All local state was WIPED on BOTH machines (deleted `~/.config/{reposync,cookiesync}/state.json` here and on yasyf@yasyf-home; backups in scratch `wipe-backup/`). So `reposync host ls --json` now returns `{"version":1,"self":"","hosts":[]}` until re-registration (the old golden no longer holds â€” that's expected). Re-register hosts/repos/browsers fresh in the new schema at/after cutover. **Don't build any compat code; use the cleanest schema.**

**Why / How to apply:** synckit is published, so PUSH synckit freely (keep its CI green on every push; move the `main` bookmark + `git push origin main` since jj keeps git HEAD detached â€” `gh repo create --push`/`jj git push --allow-new` don't work here). But the local `replace => ../synckit` means **reposync cannot build in CI or anywhere synckit isn't at `../synckit`** â€” so do NOT `jj git push` reposync during Phases 0â€“3; it would break reposync's CI. Converting reposync's replace to a real `require github.com/yasyf/synckit vX.Y.Z` + tagging synckit + reposync push + brew/PyPI all happen in **Phase 4** (release + cutover). reposync commits stay local until then.
